title: 7 Stocks to Kick Out of the S&P 500
text: There were some big changes made to the S&P 500 on March 17, with several well-known stocks getting reclassified. For instance, Target (NYSE: TGT ), Dollar General (NYDE: DG ) and Dollar Tree (NASDAQ: DLTR ) are now classified as consumer staples rather than consumer discretionary stocks.

title: Why Is Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) Down 13.1% Since Last Earnings Report?
text: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) reported earnings 30 days ago. What's next for the stock?

title: Better Buy: Royal Caribbean or Norwegian Cruise Line Stock?
text: Royal Caribbean earned more than twice as much revenue in the fourth quarter than in the same period in 2021. Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings had 24% higher onboard revenue last quarter than in 2019.

title: Here's Why Investors Should Retain Norwegian Cruise (NCLH) Now
text: Norwegian Cruise's (NCLH) emphasis on cost-saving initiatives bodes well. However, inflationary pressures and global supply chain constraints are a concern.

title: Why Cruise Stocks Popped Before Carnival's Earnings
text: Carnival reports earnings on Monday next week. Most analysts predict a big loss, but Deutsche Bank is optimistic about Carnival stock in the short term.

title: You're Right: Stocks Are Much Riskier Now (But Still Worth It)
text: If the S&P 500's swings are putting you on edge, don't think it's just you. Stocks are measurably riskier than they used to be.

title: Best Stock to Buy: Carnival Cruise Line vs. Norwegian Cruise Line vs.
text: These stocks are benefiting from an epic rebound in travel.

title: Better Buy: Lindblad Expeditions or Norwegian Cruise Line?
text: Lindblad Expeditions more than doubled its 2019 land-generated revenue last year. Norwegian drove higher revenue in Q4 than in the same period in pre-pandemic 2019.

title: Why Carnival, Norwegian, and Royal Caribbean Stocks Dropped Today
text: Don't look now, but another bank is in trouble: Credit Suisse. Investors are starting to worry that problems with banking could spread into the credit markets and make loans more expensive.

title: Why Carnival, Norwegian, and Royal Caribbean Stocks Dropped Today
text: Cruise line stocks are sinking Monday, two weeks after Norwegian Cruise reported an earnings miss and weak guidance. Investors may be wondering if Carnival Corporation will have similarly bad news when it reports earnings -- two weeks from now.

title: With Bookings at Historical Levels, Norwegian Cruise Line Stock Is Worth a Closer Look
text: Onboard revenue generation beat expectations in Q4. A lengthening booking window points to enduring demand.

title: After weathering pandemic storm, cruise-line stocks offer upside, says Stifel
text: Like the rest of the travel industry, cruise operators have weathered a pandemic-related storm over the last couple of years, but they now deserve attention from investors, says analyst firm Stifel.

title: Norwegian Cruise Line's bookings and capacity are heading in the right direction, analysts say
text: After the disruption of the COVID-19 pandemic, Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. has reached an important point on its journey back to normal operations, analysts say.

title: Norwegian Cruise Line says wealthy customers are still splashing out on cruise trips despite the sluggish economy
text: "We simply haven't seen any indication that the consumer is shying away from taking cruise vacations," its CEO Frank del Rio said Tuesday.
